intake_questions.text: Does your organisation process personal data of EU/EEA residents?
Do you have a designated Data Protection Officer (DPO)?
Have you completed a Record of Processing Activities (ROPA)?
Do you transfer personal data outside the EEA?
Has a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) been conducted for high-risk processing?

scope_template.prompt: Identify each business unit processing EU personal data
List systems, applications, and databases storing EU personal data
Confirm any processing activities explicitly excluded from GDPR scope

requirements.text: A documented lawful basis (Art.6) must exist for each processing activity in the ROPA.
requirements.evidence_expectation: ROPA with lawful basis column populated; legal analysis for each processing purpose

requirements.text: Privacy notices must comply with Art.13/14 requirements including identity of controller, purposes, legal basis, rights, and retention periods.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Current privacy notice; notice change log; Art.13 checklist assessment

requirements.text: Processes must be in place to respond to data subject rights requests (access, rectification, erasure, portability, objection) within statutory timeframes.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Rights request procedure; sample fulfilled requests; SLA evidence; suppression list

requirements.text: Technical and organisational security measures appropriate to the risk must be implemented and documented per Art.32.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Security policy; encryption evidence; access control review; penetration test summary

requirements.text: A documented personal data breach response procedure must be in place enabling 72-hour supervisory authority notification per Art.33.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Breach response procedure; breach register; detection mechanism evidence; notification template

requirements.text: Data Protection Impact Assessments must be conducted for high-risk processing activities and DPO consulted where required.
requirements.evidence_expectation: DPIA register; completed DPIAs; DPO consultation records; high-risk processing list

evidence_requests.title: Record of Processing Activities (ROPA)
evidence_requests.description: Complete ROPA with lawful basis, data categories, retention periods, and recipients for each processing activity

evidence_requests.title: Privacy Notice / Policy
evidence_requests.description: Current external-facing privacy notice with version history and Art.13/14 compliance checklist

evidence_requests.title: Data Subject Rights Procedure
evidence_requests.description: SOP for handling SAR, erasure, portability, and objection requests including sample fulfilled requests

evidence_requests.title: Technical Security Measures Evidence
evidence_requests.description: Encryption at rest/transit evidence, access control review, and most recent penetration test executive summary

evidence_requests.title: Breach Response Procedure and Register
evidence_requests.description: Documented breach detection-to-notification procedure and breach register (redacted as needed)

evidence_requests.title: DPIA Register and Sample DPIA
evidence_requests.description: DPIA register listing all high-risk activities and a completed DPIA for the highest-risk processing

task_templates.title: Review ROPA for completeness and lawful basis coverage
Assess privacy notice against GDPR Art.13/14 checklist
Test data subject rights request end-to-end process
Evaluate technical and organisational security measures
Verify breach detection and 72-hour notification capability
Interview DPO on DPIA process and high-risk activities
Map cross-border transfers and verify adequacy mechanisms

finding_templates.title_pattern: Missing or inadequate lawful basis for {processing_activity}
Privacy notice missing required Art.13 element: {element}
Data subject {right_type} request process not fit for purpose

severity_model.critical: Fundamental rights and freedoms at high risk; immediate regulatory action likely
severity_model.high: Significant non-compliance; supervisory authority likely to investigate
severity_model.medium: Partial compliance gap; remediation required within 90 days
severity_model.low: Minor gap or best-practice recommendation; 6-month remediation window
severity_model.informational: Observation for continuous improvement; no compliance risk

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Lawful Basis Selection Advisory
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Guide client through Art.6 basis selection for each processing purpose; document rationale

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Consent Mechanism Review
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review consent UI, granularity, and withdrawal mechanism against GDPR requirements

advisory_clinic_templates.title: DPIA Scoping Session
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Identify high-risk processing requiring DPIA; walk through screening criteria
